metadata.description: Native-first, low-friction companion plugins for Claude Code.

plugins.name: task-queue
plugins.description: Makes Claude Code's native task list a live work queue via event-driven hooks. A SessionStart hook primes the model to treat the native list as the queue, re-surfaces earlier sessions' unfinished tasks, and hydrates from a committed roadmap. A per-prompt hook runs an interpret→decompose→queue review loop — leaning to a one-line pointer once the policy is recorded in CLAUDE.md, and escalating to a present-and-approve (with a wireframe design preview) on consequential or visual work. A Stop hook checks the delivered outcome against the request. Autopilot mode runs fully autonomous when the owner steps away: it auto-continues the queue, hard-blocks questions, and parks the decisions the owner will want — important direction/design choices and irreversible/externally-binding actions — as a reviewable ❓ pile while deciding the routine, low-stakes rest itself. Per-feature slash commands (/task-queue:autopilot, checkpoint, agents, resume, ship, status) give deterministic control: resume picks up an earlier session (restore crashed edits + reinstate its tasks) and ship takes verified work from working tree to squash-merged-on-main. Plus an opt-in crash-checkpoint (working-tree snapshots to a hidden git ref) and agent fan-out. Read-only over ~/.claude/tasks (it writes only its own cache state + the optional checkpoint ref).

plugins.name: tidy
plugins.description: Tidy-as-you-touch: after each edit it formats and lint-checks just the file you touched (fixing only what's safe), surfaces its blast radius (the dependents to cover with tests), and flags it for decomposition past a size budget — keeping an actively-worked project trending down in complexity. Findings-only edit-time linters run the project's own tools for Go (goimports/gofumpt + golangci-lint), web (eslint incl. jsx-a11y, stylelint), Python (ruff) and shell (shellcheck). A Stop hook is the verification floor: when the working tree changed and the project has a discoverable test command, it runs the tests and blocks until they pass (bounded) — the safety net a non-technical owner can't produce. After a clean verify the same hook enforces the project's own quality gates (typecheck / a11y / architecture scripts), a coverage ratchet + a narrow regression gate on scar-tissue files, an import-cycle check, and a debt-prune nudge past a threshold — all detect-and-run (installs/invents nothing), with any cuts routed through the task-queue review loop. Goes quiet (one-line re-anchor) once recorded in the project's CLAUDE.md.

plugins.name: charter
plugins.description: At SessionStart, a compact project brief that flags missing Claude-facing docs (project map, roadmap/backlog, recorded decisions), injects git-derived "scar tissue" hotspots (files repeatedly fixed), and reminds the model of the non-technical owner loop. Adds a Stop-time alignment gate that checks a finished change against recorded decisions, plus the /charter:align command to reconcile open work on demand. Read-only over the project.

plugins.name: hud
plugins.description: A consolidated, read-only status line for the companion plugins, grouped into three zones split by a dim divider: [health beacon · ✓/✗ tests · disabled-safety-floors 🛡✗ · ❓ parked-decisions/open-questions] │ [feature modes — ✈️ autopilot · 🤖 agents · 🧷 logs, each icon-led, green on / grey off] │ [model · token throughput ⇡in ⇣out · git branch with dirty */ahead ↑/behind ↓]. Rendered from existing plugin state plus the statusLine payload — no hooks, no scanning, zero model-token cost; empty slots and whole zones collapse when absent, and it sheds low-priority slots on a narrow terminal. It deliberately doesn't re-render signals shown elsewhere (the task list Claude Code renders natively; docs-health charter nudges at session start). Sets no refreshInterval (the beacon is static, so it stays battery-friendly). Run /hud:setup once to wire it into settings.json with a version-resilient command.
